title: Register teach command in main.py _COMMANDS dict
description: src/folio/cli/main.py line 16: teach is not in _COMMANDS dict. Running 'folio teach' results in 'Unknown command: teach'. teach.py exists at src/folio/cli/teach.py but is undiscoverable.

title: Add --dry-run and --json to guide.py CLI
description: src/folio/cli/guide.py lines 367-421: guide CLI is missing --dry-run and --json flags required by AGENTS.md Rule 4. --dry-run should show topic list without printing full content. --json should output guide sections as structured JSON for agent consumption. Also refactor to use argparse instead of manual argv parsing.

title: Add --dry-run to scan.py CLI
description: src/folio/cli/scan.py lines 22-178: scan CLI is missing --dry-run flag required by AGENTS.md Rule 4. Scan is inherently read-only but should still accept --dry-run for agent compatibility (no-op is acceptable since scan is already a preview).

title: Add --dry-run to audit.py CLI
description: src/folio/cli/audit.py lines 20-144: audit CLI is missing --dry-run flag required by AGENTS.md Rule 4. Add --dry-run that previews the audit scope (files found, checks to run) without executing analysis.

title: Add --dry-run and --json to init.py CLI
description: src/folio/cli/init.py lines 19-109: init CLI is missing --dry-run and --json flags required by AGENTS.md Rule 4. Add both flags. --dry-run should show what would be created without writing files. --json should output structured result.

title: CLI compliance: missing --dry-run and --json flags
description: AGENTS.md Rule 4 requires every tool to have --dry-run and --json. Four CLIs are missing flags: audit.py (--dry-run), scan.py (--dry-run), init.py (--dry-run, --json), guide.py (--dry-run, --json). teach.py missing both but is a stub.

title: Add public context-building API to core.skills (replace private _build_context import)
description: src/folio/cli/skills.py line 66: imports private function _build_context from folio.core.skills. Private-function import across module boundaries violates encapsulation. Add a public build_context() or expose context via generate_skills() dry-run mode instead.

title: Clean up scan.py duplicated config-loading logic
description: src/folio/cli/scan.py lines 67-82: Config loading is duplicated with an unnecessary if/else branch on args.json_output or args.output. The only difference is whether to print a warning. Simplify to a single config-loading path with a conditional warning regardless of output mode.

title: Refactor guide.py to use argparse instead of manual argv parsing
description: src/folio/cli/guide.py lines 367-401: guide.py uses manual while-loop argv parsing instead of argparse. This is inconsistent with all other 11 CLI files which use argparse. Refactor to use argparse for consistency, discoverability, and auto-generated --help.

title: Move classify config merging from CLI into core module
description: src/folio/cli/classify.py lines 81-90: Config-merging logic (building classify_config dict from DEFAULT_CLASSIFY_CONFIG + config.funders + config.classification + config.doc_types) is business logic leaking into the CLI layer. AGENTS.md says CLIs should be thin wrappers around core. Extract into a build_classify_config() function in core/classifier.py or config/.

title: Remove dead/no-op code blocks in CLI files
description:
Multiple CLI files have dead or no-op code:
- scan.py:103-104: 'if args.output and not args.json_output: pass' — does nothing
- audit.py:71-73: 'if isinstance(audit_cfg, dict): pass else: audit_cfg = None' — the pass branch is a no-op
- clean.py:79-83: 'if isinstance(cleaner_config, dict): cleaner_config = cleaner_config else: cleaner_config = {}' — self-assignment is a no-op
Remove these dead branches.

title: Normalize config-required vs config-optional behavior across CLI stubs
description: Inconsistent config behavior across CLIs: clean.py/classify.py/audit.py/canonicalize.py silently continue when folio.yaml is missing; rewrite.py/prioritize.py/ingest.py error out with message. Decide on a project-wide convention (probably: config is required when the command can't function without LLM/paths; optional when it can work with defaults) and implement consistently.
